title: plectranthus welwitschii
intro: Plectranthus welwitschii, also known as Wild Sage, is a perennial herb native to tropical Africa, characterized by its fragrant leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Plectranthus welwitschii include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to soothe respiratory and gastrointestinal problems.
